reasons: Lolita can block ranged basic attacks and projectiles, which reduces Melissa's poke and teamfight damage windows.
Guardian's Bulwark helps protect allies when Melissa links targets and tries to attack through Muddles.

proof.summary: Melissa wants to deal sustained ranged damage through basic attacks and Muddles pressure, while Lolita can block incoming ranged basic attacks and projectiles for her team.
worksBestWhen: Lolita angles her shield between Melissa and priority allies.
Lolita saves shield for Melissa's real damage window.
failureCases: Melissa attacks from a side angle.
Lolita drops shield too early or gets displaced.

reasons: Pharsa can attack Melissa from outside Melissa's comfortable Go Away zone.
Long-range burst forces Melissa to reposition instead of freely holding lane or teamfight space.

proof.summary: Melissa is strong against enemies that must enter her repel zone, while Pharsa can damage her from long range without committing into Go Away.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa casts from safe terrain or behind frontline.
Pharsa targets Melissa before Melissa can lifesteal or reposition.
failureCases: Melissa dodges air strikes and closes with team support.
Pharsa is caught while channeling or repositioning.

reasons: Xavier can pressure Melissa from outside normal marksman trade range.
Mystic Field and Dawning Light punish Melissa when she is stationary during damage output.

proof.summary: Melissa wants extended basic-attack trades, while Xavier can poke from far range and punish her positioning without entering her self-peel circle.
worksBestWhen: Xavier keeps distance and plays behind vision.
Xavier follows ally crowd control with Mystic Field or Dawning Light.
failureCases: Melissa dodges skillshots and forces Xavier into short-range fights.
Xavier is flanked by assassins while focusing Melissa.

reasons: Franco can punish Melissa before she sets up Go Away.
A hook or suppression can drag Melissa out of her safe marksman formation.

proof.summary: Melissa wants to maintain controlled spacing, while Franco can break that spacing by forcing a pickoff before she starts her repel zone.
worksBestWhen: Franco hooks from fog or after Melissa uses mobility.
failureCases: Melissa blocks the engage with Go Away or stays behind minions.
Franco misses hook and gives Melissa free damage time.

reasons: Lesley can trade with long-range burst instead of walking into Melissa's short self-peel zone.
Her true-damage poke can punish Melissa before Melissa establishes sustained DPS.

proof.summary: Melissa wants repeated linked attacks, while Lesley can use longer-range poke windows to damage her without committing to an extended trade.
worksBestWhen: Lesley pokes when Lethal Shot is ready.
Lesley avoids being linked by Muddles for extended trades.
failureCases: Melissa lands Muddles and forces Lesley into continuous DPS exchange.
Lesley is caught without camouflage or spacing.
